A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH STRUCK THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF HOPKINSVILLE DURING THE PREDAWN HOURS OF TUESDAY, JANUARY 3RD. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS IN THE FORM OF SEVERAL LARGE SNAPPED TREE LIMBS. A CINDER BLOCK GARAGE ATTACHED TO A HOME OFF COX MILL ROAD HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED, WITH THE ADJACENT PORTION OF THE HOME HAVING A SMALL PORTION OF ITS ROOF REMOVED AS WELL. A LARGE CARPORT WAS LIFTED AND TOSSED AT LEAST 25 YARDS INTO A WOODED AREA NEAR WOODMONT DRIVE.

DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES, INCLUDING ROOF AND SIDING LOSS. MANY TREES SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED. MULTIPLE METAL ROOF PANELS BLOWN OFF TWO LARGE BARNS. MINOR FENCE DAMAGE AT A CHURCH. DAMAGED HOMES AND TREES DOWN IN THE FREMONT AREA REPORTED BY OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 NNE BOAZ. AN EF-2 TORNADO BEGAN AT 1108 AM CST JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF OLD MAYFIELD ROAD AND MCNEIL ROAD. TRAVELING NNE, THE TORNADO PARALLELED OLD MAYFIELD ROAD, DOING SIGNIFICANT ROOF, SIDING, AN FASCIA DAMAGE TO ROUGHLY TWO DOZEN HOMES. A COUPLE HOMES HAD MOST OF THE THEIR ROOFING STRUCTURE REMOVED COMPLETELY. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. NUMEROUS WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED OR TILTED BY TORNADIC WINDS. AFTER CROSSING HARDMONEY ROAD, THE TORNADO DID DAMAGE TO HOMES, TREES, AND OUTBUILDINGS ALONG MICHAEL DRIVE WEST BEFORE DISSIPATING AROUND 1110 AM CST. DOZENS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, AND DOZENS MORE SUFFERED MAJOR TREE LIMB DAMAGE. ADDITIONALLY, STRONG INFLOW WINDS OF 80 TO 90 MPH CAUSED TREE DAMAGE AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ADJACENT TO THE TORNADO PATH ALONG MICHAEL DRIVE EAST.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN GREENVILLE/CENTRAL CITY AREA.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 WSW REIDLAND. DAMAGED HOMES AND TREES DOWN IN THE FREMONT AREA REPORTED BY OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE. TIME DETERMINED FROM RADAR.

POLE BARN DAMAGED TREES DOWN.

SEVERAL BARNS DESTROYED. ESTIMATED AT LEAST 60 MPH WINDS.

AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE STURGIS MUNICIPAL AIRPORT IN UNION COUNTY, KY. TRAVELING NNE, THE TORNADO PARTIALLY DEMOLISHED THREE SEPARATE LARGE BARNS ALONG ITS PATH. THE TORNADO ALSO SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES AND DOWNED DOZENS OF LARGE TREE LIMBS. THE TORNADO ROUGHLY PARALLELED POPLAR RIDGE ROAD INITIALLY. AFTER CROSSING KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 270, THE TORNADO THEN PASSED ALONG KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 1177 BEFORE DISAPPEARING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF KENTUCKY HIGHWAYS 1177 AND 758, JUST NORTHWEST OF THE COMMUNITY OF PRIDE, KY.

AN EF-1 TORNADO STRUCK FAR WESTERN UNION COUNTY. MOVING NNE, THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF IV RUNYON ROAD AND GEIGER LAKE ROAD. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL TREES IN THIS AREA AS WELL AS PRODUCING MINOR ROOF AND FASCIA DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF HOMES. WHEN THE TORNADO REACHED THE INTERSECTION OF KENTUCKY HIGHWAYS 56 AND 109, IT REMOVED A PORTION OF THE ROOF FROM A LARGE OUTBUILDING AND PARTS OF ROOFING AND FASCIA FROM A SMALL CONVENIENCE STORE. JUST NORTH OF THE HIGHWAY 56, THE TORNADO SNAPPED APPROXIMATELY FIVE LARGE HARDWOOD TREES AND DOWNED SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS BEFORE DISSIPATING.

A WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TRAVERSED THE OHIO RIVER BOTTOMLANDS IN FAR WESTERN UNION COUNTY, KY. THE DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO DOWNED LARGE TREE LIMBS SCATTERED ALONG A PATH FROM GOOSE POND ROAD, EXTENDING NORTH ALONG KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 871, ENDING JUST TO THE NORTHEAST OF THE COMMUNITY OF RALEIGH, KY.
BARN DAMAGED ON HWY 56, SHED DAMAGED AT WMSK RADIO STATION, A ROOF GONE OFF A HOME ON THE UNION/HENDERSON COUNTY LINE.

AN EF-1 TORNADO PASSED JUST TO THE WEST OF THE SMITH MILLS, KY, COMMUNITY IN HENDERSON COUNTY. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED ALONG JJ UTLEY ROAD. TRAVELING NNE ALONG GREENLICK BAPTIST CHURCH ROAD, THE TORNADO DESTROYED A FARM OUTBUILDING. THE TORNADO CROSSED KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 359 JUST SOUTH OF SMITH MILLS WHERE SEVERAL TREES SUFFERED EXTENSIVE LARGE LIMB DAMAGE. CONTINUING NNE, THE TORNADO PASSED THROUGH A CEMETERY ALONG GRANDPA JONES ROAD WHERE SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO DIMINISHED AS IT REACHED KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 136 JUST WEST OF SMITH MILLS.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 4 SW SPOTTSVILLE. HOUSE WITH MAJOR ROOF SHINGLE LOSS ON HWY 351 JUST EAST OF ZION. FURTHER DOWN THE ROAD ON HWY 351, A LARGE BARN LOST PART OF ITS ROOF.
HOUSE WITH MAJOR ROOF SHINGLE LOSS ON HWY 531 JUST EAST OF ZION. FURTHER DOWN THE ROAD ON HWY 531, A LARGE BARN LOST PART OF ITS ROOF.
ROOF BLOWN OFF ST JOE CATHOLIC CHURCH IN ST JOE. MULTIPLE UTILITY LINES AND POLES DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL.
TREE 20INCH IN DIAMETER BLOWN OVER. A FEW SECTIONS OF ROOFING ON A HOUSE PEELED UP TO SUB-ROOFING. SCATTERED FENCING DOWN.
MESONET STATION CCTY GREENVILLE KY 6 N.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF ST JOSEPH AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2.4 MILES, LIFTING 0.4 MILES WEST OF ST JOSEPH. PEAK WINDS WERE 100 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. TREE AND MINOR HOUSE DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND FASCIA OCCURRED. IN ST. JOSEPH, THE TORNADO DID ITS MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WHEN IT REMOVED THE ROOF OF ST. JOSEPH CATHOLIC CHURCH. DOZENS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AT THE BASE.
TREES DOWN.
MOBILE HOME HOME LIFTED AND MOVED ABOUT 4 TO 5 FT. NEARBY CARPORT MOVED INTO NEIGHBORS YARD. SEVERAL BARNS DESTROYED IN THE AREA.
SHINGLES OFF ROOFS, SOME SIDING OFF OF HOUSES AND TREES DOWN.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1.5 MILES NORTH OF DARMSTADT AND LIFTED 3.3 MILES NORTH-NORTHEAST OF DARMSTADT. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.9 MILES, WITH PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH, AND MAX WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SEVERAL DOWNED LARGE TREE LIMBS AND MINOR ROOF AND FASCIA DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES ALONG DARMSTADT ROAD AND OLD PRINCETON ROAD.
AWOS STATION KHOP FORT CAMPBELL KY.
SHINGLES OFF ROOF, SIDING OFF HOUSE, TREES DOWN.
TREES DOWN IN ROCKPORT. 3 POWER POLES DOWN ALONG WITH TREES DOWN ON HWY 161 JUST NORTH OF OWENSBORO.
SEMI OVERTURNED AT MILE MARKER 58 ON I-64.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 0.9 MILES EAST-SOUTHEAST OF DALE, INDIANA AND LIFTED 4 MILES WEST-NORTHWEST OF FERDINAND. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 5.6 MILES ACROSS SPENCER AND DUBOIS COUNTIES. PEAK WINDS WERE 95 MPH AND MAX WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. A LARGE BARN HAD HALF OF ITS ROOF REMOVED. A NEARBY HOUSE HAD ITS WEST FACING WINDOWS BLOWN IN, SUFFERED SHINGLE AND FASCIA DAMAGE, AND AN ADJACENT TV ANTENNA WAS BENT AT ITS BASE PARALLEL TO THE GROUND. CROSSING INTERSTATE 64, THE TORNADO OVERTURNED A SEMITRAILER ABOUT ONE MILE EAST OF THE INTERCHANGE WITH US 231. ALONG THE SPENCER-DUBOIS COUNTY LINE, SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE OR UPROOTED. ADDITIONALLY, A COUPLE OF HOMES RECEIVED MINOR ROOF AND FASCIA DAMAGE.
SEVERAL TREE LIMBS DOWN, SOME AS LARGE AS 6â IN DIAMETER.

*** 1 INJ *** SCATTERED LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN. SEVERAL SHALLOW-ROOTED TREES UPROOTED. TWO 2-STORY BUILDINGS DOWNTOWN HAD WINDOWS BLOWN OUT WHICH CAUSED THE OUTER WALLS TO COLLAPSE. SEVERAL TREES ON HOMES CAUSING MAJOR DAMAGE. MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES INCLUDING SIDING, SHINGLES, AND GUTTER DAMAGE. WINDS WERE 80-85 MPH IN MOST PLACES WITH ISOLATED SPOTS REACHING 90 MPH.
DAMAGE TO THE MIXER RESTAURANT IN DOWNTOWN HOPKINSVILLE. THE THE THIRD STORY OF THE BUILDING COLLAPSED.
LARGE TREE DOWN INTO POWER LINES NEAR PERDUE LANE AND S VIRGINIA ST IN HOPKINSVILLE.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN INITIALLY IN EXTREME NORTHEAST CARTER COUNTY NEAR INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY A AND COUNTY ROAD 3551. IT THEN MOVED INTO WAYNE COUNTY AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR A TOTAL OF 6.5 MILES. PEAK WINDS OF 110 MPH. SEVERAL HUNDRED TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

AN EF-1 TORNADO STRUCK PORTIONS OF FAR SOUTHWEST WAYNE COUNTY IN THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF APRIL 5. SEVERAL HUNDRED TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG HIGHWAY A AND HIGHWAY 49. ONE GARAGE SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGED BY A FALLING TREE. THE TORNADO CAUSED SHINGLE DAMAGE TO A HOUSE LOCATED ALONG HIGHWAY A.

*** 5 FATAL, 4 INJ *** A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 130 MPH STRUCK BOLLINGER COUNTY, MISSOURI, EARLY WEDNESDAY MORNING APRIL 5TH. SEVERAL MANUFACTURED HOMES WERE TOTALLY DESTROYED. ADDITIONALLY, NUMEROUS HOMES SUFFERED MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, INCLUDING ROOF DAMAGE, BROKEN WINDOWS AND COLLAPSE OF WALLS. THE MOST SEVERE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN GLENALLEN, MISSOURI, WHERE MULTIPLE FATALITIES AND INJURIES OCCURRED. SEVERE TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE VICINITY OF GLENALLEN, WHERE HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE. DOZENS OF FARM OUTBUILDINGS SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.
MULTIPLE HOMES DAMAGED. UNKNOWN IF THERE ARE ANY INJURIES OR FATALITIES AT THIS TIME.
PEA SIZED HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.
ASOS STATION KMDH CARBONDALE IL.

INITIAL DAMAGE ASSESSMENT. 12 STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED INCLUDING 1 POULTRY BARN THAT WAS A TOTAL LOSS. SCATTERED TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN.
TREE ON TRAILER. CHURCH HAS MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE. TREE ON CARPORT AND TRUCK. CARPORT LIFTED IN THE AIR STRUCK 2 HOUSES AND A POWER POLE.

A SHORT LIVED EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG HIGHWAY 286 IN BALLARD COUNTY DURING THE AFTERNOON OF APRIL 5. THE MOST SEVERE DAMAGE WAS TO A RESIDENCE LOCATED ALONG HIGHWAY 286, WHICH SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT SHINGLE, GUTTER, AND FASCIA DAMAGE AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO AN AIR CONDITIONING UNIT AND AN ATTACHED PORCH. SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE ALSO DOWNED BY THE TORNADO IN THE VICINITY OF THE RESIDENCE.
TREE DOWN WITH POWER LINES AND BROKEN POLE.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED IN CALVERT CITY ON THE AFTERNOON OF APRIL 5. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE AROUND 95 MPH. ONE BARN COLLAPSED. NUMEROUS HOMES HAD SHINGLE DAMAGE. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED.
MEASURED ON TOP OF KENTUCKY DAM.

EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 MILES WNW OF MURRAY NEAR STELLA AND LIFTED 3.3 MILES NORTH OF MURRAY. PATH LENGTH OF 5.3 MILES AND PEAK WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. MAX WINDS OF 85 MPH. SEVERAL HOMES WITH SHINGLE DAMAGE. ONE GARAGE SLID OFF ITS FOUNDATION. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. ROOF DAMAGE AND WINDOWS BLOWN IN AT A COMMERCIAL SITE. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG POOR FARM ROAD.
POLES AND POWER LINES DOWN. DEBRIS ON FIELDS AND ROADSIDES.
WIRES DOWN ON KANSAS ROAD & SEVERAL TREES DOWN ON KREMER ROAD.

AN EF0 TORNADO IMPACTED PORTIONS OF RURAL SPENCER COUNTY DURING THE AFTERNOON HOURS OF APRIL 5. THE DAMAGE OCCURRED SOUTHEAST OF CHRISNEY. THE PRIMARY DAMAGE CAUSED BY THE TORNADO WAS DOWNING SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS AS WELL AS SNAPPING OR UPROOTING A FEW TREES. THE TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN THE WALLS OF A BARN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 700 NORTH AND OLD US HIGHWAY 231.

*** 2 INJ *** A brief EF-2 tornado struck a homestead near the community of Bertrand in Scott County, MO. The tornado started on County Highway 532 just west of the intersection of Highway O. The tornado shifted and damaged several grain bins and destroyed two barns. A person in a truck nearby had their vehicle partially lifted briefly by the tornado. After crossing Highway O, the tornado severely damaged a house and adjacent auxiliary home, removing the roofs and collapsing some exterior walls of both buildings. Two people suffered minor injuries in the primary residence when they were cut by flying debris. After damaging some trees behind the buildings, the tornado dissipated as it moved into an agricultural field east of the residences.

Storm damage survey results confirm an EF-1 tornado began in the Terrace Drive and Sunset Street area of Hickman with damage to trees and power lines. This tornado tracked to the ESE with more tree and power line damage. A large tree fell onto a home on Myron Cory Drive. The tornado was at its widest when crossing KY-125 and 7th Street, the latter of which had the most significant cluster of damage with several trees uprooted, portions of a few trees snapped, and power line damage. One of the trees fell onto a car. Nelson Street marked the last damage points, with the tornado lifting to the east.

Widespread tree and power line damage countywide. Trees on houses and roofs off outbuildings. Cities of Fulton and Hickman both with significant damage.

Storm damage survey results confirm an EF-1 tornado began with a cluster of damage along KY-307 just south of Johnson Rd, including on Bizzle Rd and Laine Rd. Around two dozen trees were blown down and a grain bin was destroyed on Laine Rd. This was the strongest and widest portion of the 2.6 mile long tornado track. The tornado proceeded northeastward, with damage to a portion of a tree line south of Johnson Rd. Isolated large branch and small tree limb damage was found further northeast. The last damage point was a large section of a tree falling onto a home on Wilson Rd. The tornado appears to have lifted shortly after.
Large tree down blocking Hwy 116 west of Fulton. Also brought down some power lines.
Kentucky Transportation cabinet reports numerous trees down across Hickman County. Trees are down along KY 307, KY 58, KY 239, KY 1529, and KY 1708.
Several trees down on County line road just northwest of Water Valley.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 ESE Glenallen. Water in buildings in Glenallen. Water flowing 3 to 4 feet deep in Marble Hill. Water into numerous campers at Twin Bridges Park. Several roadways washed out.
Significant flooding in and around Glenallen. Up to 6 ft feet of water flowing in some locations. Highway 34 was shut down as a nearby creek flowed over it.
Flooding is occurring near the main bridge in Marble Hill. The water is starting to recede at the time of report.
Significant flooding near the intersection of Hwy Y and Hwy 34. Water rescues occurring at the Castor River Campground.
16 individuals were rescued from floodwaters through the county. At one point 22 county highways and roads were flooded.

Continuous 13.2 mile track from west of Guthrie at the state line to south of Allensville at the Logan County line. Worst damage consistent with 125 mph, EF-2 intensity. Part of a longer-tracked tornado.
